description:
You are a compliance analyst who must produce an auditable trail of several basic network-diagnostic commands that you run on the current machine.  
  Create ONE self-contained directory that holds every artefact of your work so that an external audit bot can verify it later.

  1. Directory location  
     • Full path:  /home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics  
     • Create it if it does not already exist.  
     • All files described below must live directly inside this directory (no nested sub-folders).

  2. Diagnostics you must run – in the **exact order** given below  
     a. ping -c 2 127.0.0.1  
     b. ping -c 2 1.1.1.1  
     c. traceroute -n 127.0.0.1          (if traceroute is missing, still attempt it so the failure is captured)  
     d. ss -lnt                           (lists listening TCP sockets)  

  3. How to capture the evidence  
     • For every command, immediately capture:  
       – a RFC 3339 timestamp *before* the command is launched,  
       – the literal command string,  
       – the exit status ($?),  
       – full STDOUT,  
       – full STDERR.  

  4. Raw log file (raw_log.txt) – required exact format  
     • File path: /home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics/raw_log.txt  
     • Append a **command block** for each of the four diagnostics, exactly like the template below (replace the items in «…»):  

           --- COMMAND ---
           timestamp: «YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ»
           cmd: «the exact command you ran»
           exit_status: «numeric exit status»
           stdout_start
           «every line of STDOUT exactly as produced»
           stdout_end
           stderr_start
           «every line of STDERR exactly as produced»
           stderr_end
           --- END COMMAND ---

     • The five delimiter lines  
       --- COMMAND ---  
       stdout_start  
       stdout_end  
       stderr_start  
       stderr_end  
       --- END COMMAND ---  
       must appear **exactly** as written (all lower-case, single spaces, three dashes).

  5. Machine-readable summary (summary.json)  
     • File path: /home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics/summary.json  
     • JSON array containing one object per command, in the same order as run.  
     • Each object must have these keys (all lower-case):  
       "command"        – string (the exact command)  
       "exit_status"    – integer  
       "stdout_lines"   – integer (how many lines of STDOUT)  
       "stderr_lines"   – integer (how many lines of STDERR)  
     • Example shape (values are illustrative):  

         [
           {
             "command": "ping -c 2 127.0.0.1",
             "exit_status": 0,
             "stdout_lines": 8,
             "stderr_lines": 0
           },
           …
         ]

  6. Integrity manifest (integrity.sha256)  
     • File path: /home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics/integrity.sha256  
     • Produce SHA-256 checksums for the two files you created above, exactly in the format output by the `sha256sum` utility:  

           <checksum1>  raw_log.txt
           <checksum2>  summary.json

     • The audit bot will later run `sha256sum -c integrity.sha256` inside the directory, so make sure the manifest verifies cleanly.

  7. Permissions  
     • Ensure that the directory and all three files are readable by everyone and writable by you:  
       – Directory: 0755  
       – Files:     0644  

  8. Validation expectations  
     When the audit bot evaluates your work it will confirm:  
     • The directory exists at the exact path given.  
     • raw_log.txt contains four command blocks in the order listed, with all delimiter lines present.  
     • Each block’s “cmd:” line matches exactly the command sequence above.  
     • summary.json is valid JSON, has four objects, and the numeric counts in each object match what appears in raw_log.txt.  
     • integrity.sha256 passes `sha256sum -c`.  

  Follow the steps, keep the required formats byte-for-byte, and your audit trail will pass automated compliance checks.
truth:
<!-- Verification data used by the hidden test harness -->

  <!-- Directory that must be created -->
  <dir>/home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics</dir>

  <!-- Files that must be created -->
  <files>
    <file name="raw_log.txt" path="/home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics/raw_log.txt"/>
    <file name="summary.json" path="/home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics/summary.json"/>
    <file name="integrity.sha256" path="/home/user/audit_trails/2023-Q4-Network-Diagnostics/integrity.sha256"/>
  </files>

  <!-- The harness will look for exactly four command blocks -->
  <expected_command_order>
    ping -c 2 127.0.0.1
    ping -c 2 1.1.1.1
    traceroute -n 127.0.0.1
    ss -lnt
  </expected_command_order>

  <!-- Delimiter strings that must appear -->
  <delimiters>
    --- COMMAND ---
    --- END COMMAND ---
    stdout_start
    stdout_end
    stderr_start
    stderr_end
  </delimiters>

  <!-- JSON keys that must be present in every object -->
  <summary_keys>
    command
    exit_status
    stdout_lines
    stderr_lines
  </summary_keys>

  <!-- Permissions that will be verified -->
  <permissions>
    <dirmode>0755</dirmode>
    <filemode>0644</filemode>
  </permissions>

  <!-- The harness will compute sha256sum on raw_log.txt and summary.json and check them against integrity.sha256 -->
  <integrity_check>true</integrity_check>
